sections.title: Product definition
sections.text: Wheat flour is the product prepared from grain of common wheat, Triticum aestivum L., or club wheat, Triticum compactum Host., or mixtures thereof, by grinding or milling processes in which the bran and germ are partly removed and the remainder is comminuted to a suitable degree of fineness.
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION > Product definition
number=2.1
level=2
Wheat flour is the product prepared from grain of common wheat, Triticum aestivum L., or club wheat, Triticum compactum Host., or mixtures thereof, by grinding or milling processes in which the bran and germ are partly removed and the remainder is comminuted to a suitable degree of fineness.

page_spans.text: Wheat flour is the product prepared from grain of common wheat, Triticum aestivum L., or club wheat, Triticum compactum Host., or mixtures thereof, by grinding or milling processes in which the bran and germ are partly removed and the remainder is comminuted to a suitable degree of fineness.

sections.title: Quality factors – general
sections.text:
Wheat flour and any added ingredients shall be safe and suitable for human consumption.
Wheat flour shall be free from abnormal flavours, odours, and living insects.
Wheat flour shall be free from filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in amo unts which may represent a hazard to human health.

sections.title: Food additives
sections.text: Flour treatment agents, carriers and glazing agents used in accordance with Table 1 and Table 2 of the General standard for food additives (CXS 192-1995)1 in food category 06.2.1 (Flours) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard.

level=2

page_spans.text: Flour treatment agents, carriers and glazing agents used in accordance with Table 1 and Table 2 of the General standard for food additives (CXS 192-1995)1 in food category 06.2.1 (Flours) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard.

sections.title: Moisture content  15.5 percent m/m max
sections.text: Lower moisture limits should be required for certain destinations in relation to the climate, duration of transport and storage.

sections.title: Optional ingredients
sections.text:
The following ingredients may be added to wheat flour in amounts necessary for technological purposes:
– malted products with enzymatic activity made from wheat, rye or barley;
– vital wheat gluten; and
– soybean flour and legume flour.

CONTAMINANTS
number=5

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate secti ons of the General principles of food hygiene (CXC 1-1969)2 and other codes of practice recommended by the CAC which are relevant to this product. To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter. When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product: – shall be free from microorganisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health; – shall be free from parasites which may represent a hazard to health; and – shall not contain any substance originating from microorganisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text: Wheat flour shall be packaged in containers which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product. The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended use. They should not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product. When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.

sections.title: Labelling of non-retail containers
sections.text: Information for non -retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that suc h a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

sections.title: Factor/Description Limit Method of analysis
sections.citation: f852212b-ab58-4782-8141-48909c53cd80, Factor/Description Limit Method of analysis, p. 5
sections.text:
ASH
Buyer preference
Refer to Section 9
FAT ACIDITY
Max: 70 mg per 100 g flour on a dry matter basis expressed as sulphuric acid – or – Not more than 50 mg of potassium hydroxide shall be required to neutralize the free fatty acids in 100 grammes flour on a dry matter basis
Refer to Section 9
PROTEIN (5.7)
Min: 7.0% on a dry weight basis
Refer to Section 9
NUTRIENTS
i. vitamins
ii. minerals
iii. amino acids
Conform with legislation of the country in which the product is sold
None defined
PARTICLE SIZE (GRANULARITY)
98% or more of flour shall pass through a 212 micron (No. 70 sieve)
Refer to Section 9

sentences.original: In addition to the requirements of the General standard for the labelling of pre-packaged foods (CXS 1-1985)3 the following specific provisions apply:

classification.sentence: In addition to the requirements of the General standard for the labelling of pre-packaged foods (CXS 1-1985)3 the following specific provisions apply:

classification.justification: Normative introductory sentence setting labelling requirements beyond another standard; obligation is implied; no template match.

sentences.original: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended methods of analysis and sampling (CXS 234-1999)4 relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

classification.sentence: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended methods of analysis and sampling (CXS 234-1999)4 relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

classification.justification: Sentence imposes obligation to use designated methods for compliance verification; no known template match.

sentences.original: Wheat flour shall be free from heavy metals in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.

classification.sentence: Wheat flour shall be free from heavy metals in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.

classification.pattern: Obligation with quantitative health risk qualification

classification.justification: Clear normative prohibition from exceeding hazardous heavy metals levels; typical safety limit obligation but no matching template.

sentences.original: Wheat flour shall comply with those maximum residue limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission for this commodity.

sentences.original: Information for non -retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container.

classification.pattern: Conditional clause + main clause with advisory recommendation

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a strong recommendation with a conditional clause. No known templates match this advisory and nuanced obligation pattern.
notes: Not a direct normative obligation but strong recommendation

pages.title: STANDARD FOR WHEAT FLOUR
pages.summary: Página contém título do documento 'STANDARD FOR WHEAT FLOUR' e cabeçalho 'CXS 152-1985', sem seções ou texto adicional.

A página apresenta o cabeçalho 'CXS 152-1985  2' e uma seção principal de nível 1 titulada '2023 Amendments' com texto explicativo associado. Não há evidências de continuidade estrutural entre páginas.

A página 3 do documento apresenta o cabeçalho 'CXS 152-1985  3'. Foram identificadas quatro seções principais de nível 1 com títulos numerados e suas subseções correspondentes de níveis 2 e 3 conforme a numeração. O texto de cada seção foi associado corretamente, respeitando os limites hierárquicos e a ordem natural. Não há indicação de continuidade de conteúdo da página anterior nem de continuidade para a próxima página.

A página contém as seções numeradas de 5 a 9 do documento, estruturadas hierarquicamente conforme a numeração apresentada. O cabeçalho da página está identificado. Não há indicações de continuidade de conteúdo para a página anterior ou para a próxima página. Os textos foram associados corretamente aos títulos correspondentes, respeitando a hierarquia e a ordem natural da página.

A página contém um título principal 'ANNEX' (nível 1) seguido de uma subseção sem numeração que agrupa vários fatores e métodos de análise. Os textos estão associados corretamente aos respectivos nós, e não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas.
